summary: This white paper, primarily authored by Patrick McKenzie as part of a pseudonymous Tokyo working group, argues that Japan's official covid-19 case counts in March 2020 were massively understated, possibly by 5X or more. Using multiple lines of statistical evidence—Diamond Princess asymptomatic rates, Singapore airport detection data, and case fatality ratio modeling—the authors demonstrate that Japan's cluster containment strategy had already failed. The paper warns that absent aggressive intervention, Japan would face a national health crisis with over 500,000 infections by end of April 2020. It directly challenges the governmental and media consensus that Japan was weathering the pandemic well, calling voluntary behavioral changes insufficient.
key_points: Japan's testing policy systematically undercounted asymptomatic infections, detecting only ~10% asymptomatic cases versus the 48-50% rate observed in exhaustively tested populations like the Diamond Princess
Singapore's airport detected infections in Japan travelers at 5X the rate implied by Japan's official numbers, suggesting massive community transmission outside surveilled clusters
The cluster containment strategy had already failed, with Osaka and Nagoya reporting infections with no link to known clusters and hospitals approaching capacity
Japan was taking minimal societal precautions—shinkansen ridership down only 8%, mask wearing below 30% in central Tokyo, bars and restaurants open—while facing exponential growth
The paper projected 500,000+ true infections and healthcare system breakdown in major cities by end of April, calling it a crisis worse than any since the war
money_quotes: The governmental and media consensus is that Japan is weathering covid-19 well. This consensus is wrong.
This is not a strategy. We are already observing breakout infections.
This requires us to believe the evidence of our eyes and our instruments, not the evidence of our hopes.
The cluster containment strategy has failed. Medical care in epicenters will be modified to adjust to a worsening reality, within days.
As a nation, we believe we are in a peaceful spring. We are not.
We will, within the month of April, confront a crisis worse than any since the war.
Japan's testing capacity is underused, as a matter of policy.
If tourists are only getting affected by community transmission, this implies the official count is understated by 25X or more.
key_insight: Multiple independent statistical analyses converge on the same conclusion: Japan's official covid-19 numbers were a gross undercount, its cluster containment strategy had already failed, and the country was weeks away from a public health crisis it hadn't yet acknowledged.
